transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: First National Bank, good evening. This is Jasmine. Can I take your name and date of birth for verification?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Sure — it's Ivan Petrov, ninth of March 1982.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you Ivan, I've got you verified. What can I do for you?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: My card's been blocked out of nowhere. I was trying to pay for dinner and it was declined — very embarrassing.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm really sorry about that. Let me look into this straight away. The card was locked by our automated security system forty minutes ago — there was an attempt to use the card for an e-commerce transaction that flagged as suspicious.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I didn't make any online purchases today.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's very helpful. I've blocked the card permanently and I'm generating a new card for you. This suggests someone may have your card details. Your new card will arrive in three to five working days.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: How do I pay for things in the meantime?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You can visit any branch tomorrow with photo ID for a temporary card. I can also arrange Google Pay provisioning right now if your phone supports it — that's immediate.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Oh, the Google Pay option would be perfect.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'll send an SMS to your registered number with setup instructions right now. Anything else this evening?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: That's brilliant, thank you. Really sorted it out.
